observations.command:
python3 - <<'PY2'
import hashlib, json
from pathlib import Path
root=Path('build_program/phases/P3/candidates/P3-SPEC-LOCK-CREATION-RESULT')
manifest=json.loads((root/'CANDIDATE_MANIFEST.json').read_text())
review=json.loads((root/'REVIEW_TARGET.json').read_text())
decision=json.loads((root/'REVIEW_DECISION.json').read_text())
rows=[]
for rel in manifest['generated_outputs']:
    rows.append(f"{rel}\0{hashlib.sha256((root/rel).read_bytes()).hexdigest()}")
subject=hashlib.sha256('\n'.join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()
assert subject == review['review_subject_hash'] == decision['subject_hash']
assert 'REVIEW_DECISION.json' in manifest.get('supporting_outputs', [])
assert 'REVIEW_DECISION.json' not in manifest['generated_outputs']
assert 'VALIDATION_REPLAY_OBSERVATIONS.json' in manifest.get('supporting_outputs', [])
assert 'VALIDATION_REPLAY_OBSERVATIONS.json' not in manifest['generated_outputs']
print(subject)
PY2

python3 - <<'PY2'
import hashlib, json, subprocess
from pathlib import Path
lock_path=Path('control/locks/P3_SPEC_LOCK.json')
lock=json.loads(lock_path.read_text())
def sha(path):
    return hashlib.sha256(Path(path).read_bytes()).hexdigest()
rows=[f"{e['spec_part']}\0{e['subject_hash']}" for e in lock['files']]
review_rows=[f"{e['spec_part']}\0{e['review_decision']['sha256']}" for e in lock['files']]
assert hashlib.sha256('\n'.join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()==lock['root_sha256']
assert hashlib.sha256('\n'.join(review_rows).encode()).hexdigest()==lock['review_decision_file_root_sha256']
for entry in lock['files']:
    assert sha(entry['review_decision']['path']) == entry['review_decision']['sha256']
    for output in entry['generated_outputs']:
        assert sha(output['path']) == output['sha256']
proc=subprocess.run('python3 tooling/create_spec_lock.py --phase P3 --input-bundle build_program/phases/P3/candidates/P3-SPEC-LOCK-CREATION-RESULT/SPEC_LOCK_INPUT_BUNDLE.json --output control/locks/P3_SPEC_LOCK.json', shell=True, text=True, capture_output=True)
assert proc.returncode != 0
assert 'output already exists' in proc.stderr
print('PASS')
PY2

python3 - <<'PY2'
import json
from pathlib import Path
paths=[Path('control/locks/P3_SPEC_LOCK.json')]
root=Path('build_program/phases/P3/candidates/P3-SPEC-LOCK-CREATION-RESULT')
manifest=json.loads((root/'CANDIDATE_MANIFEST.json').read_text())
paths.extend(root / rel for rel in manifest['generated_outputs'])
paths.extend(root / rel for rel in manifest.get('supporting_outputs', []))
markers=('T'+'BD','TO'+'DO','FIX'+'ME','PLACE'+'HOLDER','TO_BE'+'_FILLED')
for path in paths:
    text=path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    if path.suffix=='.json':
        json.loads(text)
    upper=text.upper()
    assert not any(marker in upper for marker in markers), path
    for idx,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
        assert line == line.rstrip(' \t'), f'{path}:{idx}'
print('PASS')
PY2

self_reference_boundary.why_supporting_only: The replay and review decision files record final command outputs after generated outputs are stable. Keeping them outside generated_outputs prevents subject hash recursion.
